assignments.name: commands
assignments.value: [   "kubectl get nodes -o wide",   "kubectl get pods -A",   "talosctl health --nodes 192.168.68.115 --wait-timeout 10s",   "talosctl health --nodes 192.168.68.116 --wait-timeout 10s",   "talosctl health --nodes 192.168.68.117 --wait-timeout 10s" ]

nodes.name: Edit Fields
nodes.notes: Defines the list of kubectl and talosctl commands to run. Update the 3 IPs (192.168.68.115/116/117) to match your own control-plane node addresses.

nodes.name: Execute Command
nodes.notes: Runs each command in the OS shell. onError=continueErrorOutput ensures both stdout (success) and stderr (failure) feed into Merge — no output is lost.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Runs daily at 10:45 AM. Adjust triggerAtHour / triggerAtMinute to change the schedule.

nodes.name: Split Out
nodes.notes: Splits the commands array into individual items so Execute Command runs each one separately.

nodes.name: Aggregate
nodes.notes: Collects all stdout and stderr arrays from every command run into a single item that the AI Agent can analyze at once.

nodes.name: Structured Output Parser
nodes.notes: Forces the AI to return valid JSON with exactly: cluster_healthy (bool), issues_detected (bool), issue_type (string), summary (string).

nodes.name: Send a text message
nodes.notes: ⚠️ REPLACE CHAT ID: Change -1003747387698 to your own Telegram group or user chat ID. Sends a daily all-clear when the cluster is healthy.

nodes.name: Is Cluster Healthy
nodes.notes: True branch (cluster_healthy=true) → Telegram all-clear message. False branch → triggers K8s RCA AI Workflow to investigate the root cause.

nodes.name: Ollama Chat Model
nodes.notes: Uses qwen3.5:35b running locally via Ollama. Replace with any Ollama model slug. To use cloud models instead, swap this node for an OpenRouter or OpenAI Chat Model node.

parameters.text:
=From the kubectl commands:
- kubectl get nodes -o wide
- kubectl get pods -A --field-selector=status.phase!=Running
- talosctl health --nodes 192.168.68.115
- talosctl health --nodes 192.168.68.116
- talosctl health --nodes 192.168.68.117

These are the aggregated command responses:
{{ $('Aggregate').item.json.stdout }}

These commands failed or returned errors:
{{ $('Aggregate').item.json.stderr }}

Based on these results, assess the cluster health. Treat any command errors as potential indicators of node or connectivity issues.

options.systemMessage:
You are a Kubernetes diagnostics assistant responsible for monitoring and assessing the health of a Kubernetes cluster.

# Health Determination
Set cluster_healthy to:
- true → all nodes and workloads are healthy
- false → there are failing pods, nodes, storage issues, or scheduling failures

Examples of unhealthy states: CrashLoopBackOff, Pending pods, PVC stuck in Pending, node NotReady, repeated restart loops, failed image pulls, volume mount failures.

Allowed issue_type values: none | pod | deployment | node | storage | networking | scheduling | unknown

# Output Format (STRICT)
Return valid JSON only. No markdown, no extra text.
{
  "cluster_healthy": true,
  "issues_detected": false,
  "issue_type": "none",
  "summary": "All nodes are ready and all pods are running normally."
}

# Known False Positives — Do NOT flag these as issues:
- Completed CoreDNS pods typically result from terminated ReplicaSet revisions and are normal during rolling updates.
- kube-system pods with low restart counts (< 5) on a recently provisioned cluster (< 7 days) → expected during initial setup.

nodes.name: AI Agent
nodes.notes: Analyzes the aggregated command output using a local Ollama model. System prompt enforces strict JSON output and defines known false positives (e.g. completed CoreDNS pods).

nodes.name: Merge
nodes.notes: Combines the success branch (stdout) and error branch (stderr) from Execute Command so all results flow into Aggregate regardless of exit code.

nodes.name: Call 'K8s RCA AI Workflow'
nodes.notes: Triggers the K8s RCA AI Workflow when issues are detected. Passes cluster_healthy, issues_detected, issue_type, and summary as inputs for deeper investigation.

parameters.content:
## 🏥 K8s Health AI Workflow

Runs a set of **kubectl** and **talosctl** commands on a fixed schedule, feeds the raw output to a **local Ollama AI model**, and sends a **Telegram alert** based on the result.

**Flow:**
1. Triggers daily at 10:45 AM
2. Splits command list and runs each command in parallel
3. Merges + aggregates all stdout/stderr into one item
4. AI model analyzes output and returns structured JSON health report
5. If healthy → Telegram all-clear message
6. If unhealthy → triggers K8s RCA AI Workflow for root cause analysis

**Model:** Ollama (local, self-hosted) — no cloud API cost

## ⚙️ Setup

**1. Ollama**
Install Ollama and pull the model:
`ollama pull qwen3.5:35b`
Connect to the *Ollama Chat Model* node.

**2. Node IPs**
Update the 3 control-plane IPs in *Edit Fields*:
`192.168.68.115/116/117` → your node IPs

**3. Telegram Bot**
Create via @BotFather → connect to *Send a text message* node and set your chat ID

**4. K8s RCA Workflow**
Ensure the K8s RCA AI Workflow is active — this workflow calls it on failure.

## 🔄 Command Pipeline
Each command runs individually via *Split Out → Execute Command → Merge → Aggregate*.
This ensures all stdout AND stderr are captured regardless of command exit code.
